reports.title: Trump's Iran Ceasefire: Victory or Surrender?
reports.subtitle: The strongman who threatened to destroy Iran just settled for a two-week pause.

reports.title: Social Media's Tobacco Moment
reports.subtitle: A jury just declared Instagram and YouTube liable for addiction — and tech's legal immunity era might be over.

reports.subtitle: Greece bans social media for under-15s, joining a global shift where governments override parental authority over children's digital lives.

reports.title: Iran's 10-Point Plan and the Coerced Ceasefire
reports.subtitle: A hostage exchange disguised as diplomacy — both sides are trading things they can't afford to lose.

reports.title: Sánchez's Bucket and What It Costs to Say No Inside NATO
reports.subtitle: Spain's PM said the obvious thing — you don't get credit for putting out a fire you started. The interesting question is why he's the only one.
